exhibitor_details.company_address: Unit 4, Republic Park, 12 Malibongwe Drive, Ferndale, Randburg, 2194, South Africa

special_requests.special_requests:
We will be bringing a modular display system with integrated LED lighting and a small induction cooktop for live demonstrations of our energy-efficient cookware. As such, we kindly request:

1. Power requirements:
   - One (1) dedicated 220V, 32A circuit for the induction cooktop and kettle (we will use our own surge-protected extension leads and will ensure all appliances are SABS approved).
   - Two (2) additional standard 220V, 16A plug points for LED lighting, laptops, and a small receipt printer.
   - Access to power from 08:00 to 20:00 on exhibition days to allow for setup, testing, and safe shutdown.

2. Access and logistics:
   - Access for offloading via the closest service entrance to Court 2 on 2025-03-13 between 15:00 and 17:00 (a single long-wheelbase panel van – Mercedes Sprinter size).
   - Temporary parking/loading zone for approximately 60 minutes while we offload and move equipment to the stand using trolleys.
   - Permission to store empty cartons and packaging in a back-of-house area if available, or guidance on acceptable storage areas so that the stand remains uncluttered.

3. Health & safety and noise considerations:
   - We will conduct low-noise product demonstrations (short induction cooktop sessions every hour). No amplified music will be used – background audio will be kept at a conversational level only.
   - We will have two trained staff members on-site at all times who are briefed on basic fire-safety procedures, cord management, and emergency evacuation routes.
   - All display structures are freestanding and will not be fixed to walls, ceilings, or floors. We will use non-marking tape where cable taping is required.

4. Furniture and layout (if available from centre):
   - Two (2) trestle tables or equivalent (approx. 1.8m each) with black or neutral-coloured tablecloths.
   - Four (4) armless chairs for staff and customer seating during longer consultations.
   - Permission to bring our own branded pull-up banners (x3) and one fabric backdrop wall, ensuring that no fire exits or sightlines are obstructed.

5. Customer engagement and data collection:
   - We intend to run a small giveaway/competition where visitors can enter by providing their name, email address, and area of residence. All data will be collected via tablets using an online form (no paper forms) and will strictly comply with POPIA requirements. We will display a clear privacy notice explaining how data will be used (newsletter sign-ups, once-off competition communication, and opt-out options).
   - We will also offer complimentary herbal tea tasting in small biodegradable cups (50–70 ml). Ingredients will be clearly listed at the stand, and we will provide a visible disclaimer for individuals with allergies or intolerances.

6. Cleaning and waste management:
   - We will bring our own small pedal bin for tea cups and packaging, and we will separate recyclable material where possible.
   - We will ensure that the stand area is swept and tidy at the end of each day and that no liquids are spilled on the floor. Any minor spills will be cleaned immediately.

If there are any centre-specific guidelines regarding maximum stand height, fire-retardant materials, sound levels, or promotional activities (giveaways, competitions, or sampling), please share them with us so we can adjust our setup accordingly. We are happy to submit our stand layout and electrical load details for prior approval if needed.
special_requests.detailed_description_of_exhibition_inclu:
BrightLeaf Homeware (Pty) Ltd will be hosting a three-day product and service awareness exhibition focused on eco-friendly kitchen and home products aimed at urban families and young professionals. The stand is designed to be visually open and inviting, while keeping clear sightlines within Court 2 and ensuring safe pedestrian flow.

1. Overall stand footprint and layout:
   - Total requested floor area: 6m (frontage) x 3m (depth) – approximately 18 square metres.
   - Orientation: 6m side facing the main shopper flow in Court 2, with an open front and partially open sides.
   - Stand height: Main backdrop not exceeding 2.4m in height. Free-standing product plinths and banner stands will be between 2.0m and 2.1m high.

2. Structural elements:
   - Back wall: One modular fabric backdrop wall measuring 4m (width) x 2.4m (height), supported by a lightweight aluminium frame. This will display our primary branding, key product categories (induction-ready cookware, reusable food storage, and low-energy appliances), and a large QR code for online catalogue access.
   - Side elements: Two narrow shelving units (each approx. 0.8m wide x 0.4m deep x 2.0m high) placed towards the rear corners of the stand. These will hold boxed products and sample items. Shelving units are freestanding and weighted at the base for stability.
   - Front demo counter: One main demonstration counter positioned slightly off-centre towards the left, measuring approximately 1.5m (width) x 0.7m (depth) x 1.0m (height). This counter will host the induction cooktop, sample cookware, and tea-tasting station.
   - Additional tables: Up to two trestle tables (around 1.8m x 0.7m each) along the back wall, used for product displays, brochures, and a portable POS device. These will be covered with plain black tablecloths to keep the appearance cohesive.

3. Customer flow and interaction zones:
   - Open front area: A roughly 6m x 1.5m strip at the front of the stand will remain open to allow customers to step in, browse, and watch demonstrations without blocking the main mall walkway.
   - Demonstration zone: In front of the main demo counter, there will be space for 4–6 people to stand comfortably while watching the cooking demonstrations. Staff will stand behind the counter to keep hot equipment away from shoppers.
   - Consultation corner: On the right-hand side of the stand (towards the back), we plan to place a small round table (approx. 0.9m diameter) with two chairs for one-on-one product consultations, warranty explanations, and online order assistance. Tablets and brochures will be available here.

4. Electrical equipment and lighting:
   - Appliances: 1 x single-plate induction cooktop, 1 x electric kettle (for boiling water for tea samples), and 1 x small low-wattage portable hot tray for keeping prepared samples warm. All appliances are energy-efficient and SABS approved.
   - IT and devices: 2 x tablets for customer data capture and browsing the online catalogue, 1 x lightweight laptop, and 1 x small portable receipt printer.
   - Lighting: 6–8 low-wattage LED spotlights/clamps attached to the modular backdrop and shelving units to highlight hero products. No halogen lights will be used.
   - Cable management: All cables will be routed along the back of the stand where possible and taped down with non-slip, non-marking cable covers in any areas where they cross potential foot traffic.

5. Branding and visual communication:
   - Banners: Up to three pull-up banners (each approx. 0.85m x 2.0m) positioned at the back and side edges of the stand, focusing on our three main themes: Energy-Saving Cooking, Plastic-Free Pantry, and Sustainable Cleaning.
   - Signage: Clear A3-sized signs indicating show specials, bundle discounts, and competition details. A POPIA-compliant privacy notice will be placed near the tablets where data is captured.
   - Colour palette: Soft greens, neutrals, and wood tones to reinforce the eco-friendly brand message and to avoid visual clutter in the mall environment.

6. Staffing and operational details:
   - Staff numbers: 3 staff members on-site at peak times (2 at the demo counter/engagement area and 1 at the consultation table). During quieter morning periods, we may operate with 2 staff members.
   - Roles: One staff member (usually myself, Thandiwe) will focus on demonstrations and explaining product benefits with practical examples (e.g., how much electricity can be saved using induction cookware vs. conventional stovetops). Another staff member will handle product enquiries, pricing, and stock checks, while the third manages data capture for the competition and newsletter sign-ups.
   - Hours of operation: We intend to be fully operational during the mall’s trading hours for the exhibition dates, with 30–45 minutes before opening and after closing each day reserved for setup, stock checks, and cleaning.

7. Safety and compliance considerations:
   - No open flames will be used. All cooking demonstrations are limited to induction and pre-boiled water from the kettle.
   - Hot surfaces (e.g., cooking plates and pots) will be kept behind the demo counter, out of direct reach of children. Staff will verbally warn visitors about hot equipment during live demonstrations.
   - All packaging materials and spare stock will be stored neatly under tables or in closed containers at the back of the stand to prevent tripping hazards and visual clutter.

In summary, the stand is a compact, open-fronted 6m x 3m layout with a strong focus on safe, low-noise product demonstrations and customer education. It is designed to integrate smoothly into Court 2 without obstructing sightlines or emergency routes, while providing an engaging and informative experience for shoppers interested in more sustainable, energy-efficient homeware solutions.
